rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion In Support Of Defendant Frank M. Booth, Inc.S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For An Order Striking Improper Objections; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 31, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Frank M. Booth, Inc.¿s motion to compel further discovery responses, And For An Order Striking Improper Objections; and for sanctions was continued from August 25 to enable the parties to engage in further meet and confer efforts regarding the adequacy of the discovery responses served after the motion was filed. If any issues remain unresolved, the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. today specifying, without argument, those issues and appear at the hearing on August 31, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE GIFFORD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mo To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For An Order Striking Improper Objections; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 31, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Turner Construction Company¿s motion to compel further discovery responses and for sanctions appears to raise many of the same issues as the discovery motions filed by Turner Construction Co., Colgate-Palmolive Co. and Frank M. Booth, Inc. in the Ross case, all of which were continued from August 25 to August 31 to enable the parties to engage in further meet and confer efforts regarding the adequacy of the discovery responses served after the motion was filed. The court hopes that the further meet and confer efforts in the Ross case have included this motion as well. By 4pm today the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, whether they have engaged in meet and confer efforts regarding the most recent discovery responses and, if they have, specifying, without argument, the unresolved issues. Unless all issues have been resolved, the parties must appear at the hearing on August 31, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARY ALICE WILLIAMS et al VS. ALLIED PACKING & SUPPLY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Specially Appearing Defendant Union Carbide Corporations Motion To Quash Plaintiffs' Improper And Untimely Letter-Notice Of Deposition Of Plaintiff Mary Williams
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 31, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 6. Defendant B.R. Funsten & Co.¿s joinder in defendant Union Carbide Corp.¿s motion to quash plaintiffs¿ improper and untimely letter-notice of deposition of plaintiff Mary Williams is denied. The underlying motion was taken off calendar and defendant¿s joinder was untimely in that it did not provide 2 additional days for e-service. = (220/HEK)
